text: Hello. This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 你好,这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil. And I'm Bekka. Worrying about work or exams?
zh: 我是尼尔。我是贝卡。你在为工作或考试发愁吗?

text: War and climate change in the news? It's no wonder that modern life is stressful. What makes you stressed, Neil?
zh: 新闻里全是战争和气候变化的消息?难怪现代生活压力这么大。尼尔,是什么让你感到有压力?

text: Good question. I think not having enough time to do something. That really makes me stressed out. How about you?
zh: 好问题。我觉得没有足够的时间做某件事,这真的让我压力很大。你呢?

text: When I'm trying to do something that I've never tried before and I'm not very good at it, that makes me feel really stressed.
zh: 当我尝试从未做过、又做得不太好的事情时,我会感到压力很大。

text: I find that stressful too. And how do you cope with stressful situations, Neil?
zh: 我也觉得那很有压力。尼尔,你是怎么应对压力大的情况的?

text: Well, I try to slow down, pause,
zh: 嗯,我会试着放慢脚步,停顿一下,

text: take a breath and think that some point, probably later today, everything will be OK again.
zh: 深呼吸,然后想着,大概今天晚些时候,一切都会好起来的。

text: How about you? Yeah, same. But in my mind, I'm telling myself that I will be better at what I'm doing, I just need to practice more.
zh: 你呢?是啊,我也一样。不过我心里会告诉自己,我会做得更好的,只是需要多练习。

text: Many young people who feel stressed about the modern world use the word overwhelm to describe their feelings.
zh: 许多为现代世界感到有压力的年轻人,用“overwhelm”(不堪重负)这个词来形容自己的感受。

text: And overwhelm was also the topic of a recent BBC World Service programme, What in the World?
zh: “overwhelm”也是BBC世界服务近期一档节目《What in the World?》讨论的话题。

text: Here's Ikra Farooq and Claudia Hammond. So overwhelm is an emotional state where you are struggling with your current situation.
zh: 下面是伊克拉·法鲁克和克劳迪娅·哈蒙德的对话。所以,“不堪重负”是一种情绪状态,指的是你在应对当下处境时感到吃力。

text: So you might feel overloaded, you might feel you can't cope. And that might be a temporary thing,
zh: 你可能会觉得负担过重,觉得自己应付不来。这或许是暂时的,

text: hopefully, but if it's not in the longer term, if that becomes more of a kind of chronic kind of stress, then that might lead to burnout in the end.
zh: 希望如此,但如果长期如此,变成一种慢性压力,那最终可能导致“职业倦怠”。

text: Feeling overwhelmed and unable to cope with life can become chronic, meaning that it lasts for a long time.
zh: 感到不堪重负、无法应对生活可能会变成慢性状态,也就是说会持续很长时间。

text: Overwhelm can lead to burnout – mental or physical exhaustion caused by working too hard.
zh: “不堪重负”可能导致“职业倦怠”——因为工作过度而造成的身心疲惫。

text: So, how can we manage the stress in our lives and feel happier and healthier?
zh: 那么,我们该如何管理生活中的压力,变得更快乐、更健康呢?

text: That's what we'll be discussing in this episode, along with some useful new words and phrases.
zh: 这正是本期节目要讨论的内容,还会介绍一些实用的新词汇和短语。

text: You'll find all the vocabulary from this episode on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到本期的所有词汇。

text: But before we start, I have a question for you, Neil.
zh: 不过在开始之前,尼尔,我有个问题要问你。

text: One simple way of combating stress is taking time to rest.
zh: 应对压力的一个简单方法就是花时间休息。

text: So, according to research conducted by BBC
zh: 那么,根据BBC主持人兼心理学家

text: presenter and psychologist Claudia Hammond, which activity is thought to be the most restful?
zh: 克劳迪娅·哈蒙德所做的研究,哪种活动被认为最能让人放松呢?

text: Is it a reading for pleasure, b walking in nature, or c listening to music?
zh: 是A,为了消遣而阅读;B,在大自然中散步;还是C,听音乐?

text: Well, I think they're all quite relaxing, but I'm going to guess b walking in nature.
zh: 嗯,我觉得这些都挺放松的,但我猜是B,在大自然中散步。

text: I'm surprised, Neil. I know you love music. and we'll find out the answer later.
zh: 尼尔,我很意外,我知道你喜欢音乐。答案我们稍后揭晓。

text: One of the most stressful things facing many young people is exams.
zh: 许多年轻人面临的最大压力之一就是考试。

text: Here, Claudia Hammond offers some advice for managing exam stress to BBC World Service's What In The World.
zh: 在这里,克劳迪娅·哈蒙德在BBC世界服务的《What In The World》节目中,就如何应对考试压力提出了一些建议。

text: Particularly in the exam itself, if you feel stressed, then just knowing that you may well perform better
zh: 尤其是在考试当中,如果你感到紧张,那么要知道,你很可能会因此表现得更好。

text: while you're stressed, that it concentrates the mind, it sharpens, you know, nerves sharpen your thinking and allow you to really do your best.
zh: 当你紧张的时候,注意力会更集中,神经会变得敏锐,这能让你的思维更清晰,从而发挥出最好的水平。

text: It's normal to feel nerves before an exam. Nerves is an informal word for worry or anxiety.
zh: 考试前感到紧张是很正常的。“nerves”是一个非正式的词,指担忧或焦虑。

text: But according to Claudia, nerves aren't necessarily bad.
zh: 但克劳迪娅认为,紧张未必是坏事。

text: Yes, nerves can help concentrate the mind.
zh: 是的,紧张情绪可以帮助集中注意力。

text: The phrase concentrate or sharpen the mind means to think more clearly about something.
zh: “concentrate”或“sharpen the mind”这个短语的意思是让思维更清晰地思考某件事。

text: Like an actor or an athlete, feeling nerves before a big performance can help them do their best.
zh: 就像演员或运动员一样,在重要表演前感到紧张,能帮助他们发挥出最佳水平。

text: Claudia recommends other ways to reduce stress as well. well.
zh: 克劳迪娅还推荐了其他减压方法。

text: These include slowing down and accepting that it's OK to be good enough, rather than always trying for perfection.
zh: 其中包括放慢节奏,接受“足够好就行”,而不是一味追求完美。

text: Here she shares more tips with Iqra Farooq of BBC World Service's What In The World.
zh: 在这里,她与BBC世界服务《What In The World》的伊克拉·法鲁克分享了更多建议。

text: Iqra Farooq And I guess there's no one size fits all as well when it comes to this.
zh: 伊克拉·法鲁克:我想这方面也没有放之四海而皆准的办法。

text: So if someone's listening and thinking, I want some top tips on just feeling overwhelmed generally in my life, what would you say to them?
zh: 如果有听众在想,“我想要一些应对生活中普遍感到不堪重负的实用建议”,你会对他们说什么呢?

text: Iqra Farooq So first I would say accept that your to -do list is never going to end.
zh: 克劳迪娅·哈蒙德:首先,我会说,要接受你的待办事项清单永远不会有做完的一天。

text: It is always going to be there. So even, I know that might sound defeatist, but even if you get to the end of it today, tomorrow there will be something else on it.
zh: 它会一直存在。我知道这听起来可能有点悲观,但即使你今天把清单上的事都做完了,明天又会有新的事情加进去。

text: And that is just life and that is just signs of a busy life.
zh: 这就是生活,也正是忙碌生活的表现。

text: There are many ways to combat stress. Not a one -size -fits -all solution.
zh: 应对压力的方法有很多种,并没有放之四海而皆准的解决方案。

text: The phrase one -size -fits -all describes trying to apply the same solution to many different problems.
zh: “one-size-fits-all”(放之四海而皆准)这个短语用来形容试图用同一种方法解决许多不同的问题。

text: Claudia's final piece of advice is don't worry too much about your to -do list.
zh: 克劳迪娅最后给出的建议是,不要太担心你的待办事项清单。

text: The list of all the items you have to do. Why?
zh: 也就是列出所有你要做的事情的清单。为什么呢?

text: Because it never ends. Every day there will be something new to add to the list.
zh: 因为它永远不会结束。每天都会有新的事情加进清单里。

text: So why stress? Some good advice there, Neil.
zh: 那又何必焦虑呢?尼尔,这些建议真不错。

text: I think I'll try these techniques next time I feel stressed.
zh: 我想下次感到有压力时,我会试试这些方法。

text: And speaking of ways to de -stress reminds me of my question. I asked you earlier,
zh: 说到减压的方法,这让我想起了之前问你的那个问题,

text: according to research conducted by BBC presenter and psychologist Claudia Hammond, which activity is thought of to be the most restful?
zh: 根据BBC主持人兼心理学家克劳迪娅·哈蒙德所做的研究,哪种活动被认为最能让人放松?

text: And you answered B, walking in nature. Unfortunately, Neil,
zh: 你回答的是B,在大自然中散步。尼尔,很遗憾,

text: the answer is A, reading. Ah, well, reading is really relaxing as well.
zh: 正确答案是A,阅读。啊,不过阅读确实也很放松。

text: OK, let's recap the vocabulary from this episode, beginning with the adjective chronic,
zh: 好了,让我们回顾一下本期节目中的词汇,首先是形容词“chronic”(慢性的),

text: which means continuing for a long time. Burnout is a feeling of physical or mental exhaustion caused by working too hard.
zh: 意思是持续很长时间。“burnout”指因工作过度而导致的身心疲惫感。

text: If you say that a difficult situation sharpens or concentrates someone's mind, you mean that it makes them think clearly.
zh: 如果你说某个困难的处境“sharpens”或“concentrates”某人的思维,意思是它让这个人思路更清晰。

text: Nerves are an informal word for worry or anxiety.
zh: “nerves”是一个非正式说法,指担忧或焦虑。

text: A one -size -fits -all approach tries to apply the same solution to all problems rather than solving them individually.
zh: “one-size-fits-all”(一刀切)的做法是试图用同一种方案解决所有问题,而不是针对每个问题分别处理。

text: And finally, a to -do list is a list of all the tasks you have to do.
zh: 最后,“to-do list”指的是你需要完成的所有任务清单。

text: Of course, another way to beat stress is doing something you love.
zh: 当然,战胜压力的另一种方法就是去做你热爱的事情。

text: And if that's improving your English, then why not visit our website, bbclearningenglish .com,
zh: 如果提升英语正是你所热爱的,那不妨访问我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com,

text: where you'll find loads more activities for doing just that.
zh: 在那里你会找到更多帮助你提升英语的活动。

text: See you again next time, but for now it's goodbye. Bye!
zh: 我们下次节目再见,不过现在,先说再见啦,拜拜!
